SC-001 does not explicitly anchor to US-2 or US-3, though it clearly serves both. Add a citation like '(See US-2, US-3)' to the end of the requirement.
SC-002 does not explicitly anchor to US-3, though it clearly serves it. Add a citation like '(See US-3)' to the end of the requirement.

SC-004 does not explicitly anchor to US-1, though it clearly serves it. Add a citation like '(See US-1)' to the end of the requirement.
SC-005 does not explicitly anchor to US-3, though it clearly serves it. Add a citation like '(See US-3)' to the end of the requirement.

Edge Cases states 'minimum 30 images required for analysis' but User Story 1 Acceptance Scenario 1 only demonstrates 5 baseline images. This creates ambiguity: is 5 the test case or the actual minimum? The spec should clarify that 5 is a test sample while 30+ is the production requirement.

FR-004 states 'false details (present only in enhanced/reduced versions)' which contradicts the false memory paradigm. False details should be items that NEVER appeared in any version (the 'lure' items). The parenthetical should read '(items that never appeared in baseline or manipulated versions)' to match US-2's '10 false' questions.

SC-002 states '≥50 per condition' which is terminology for between-subjects designs. Since US-3 and FR-005 specify repeated-measures ANOVA (same participants across all conditions), this phrasing is confusing. Consider '≥50 complete sessions' instead.

FR-001 states 'varied baseline complexity scores' but does not define what constitutes 'varied' (e.g., minimum range, standard deviation, or quantile spread). Verifiable but needs measurable threshold for testability.

FR-002 states 'adding multiple minor objects' without defining 'multiple' (e.g., minimum count of 3-5 as referenced in US-1). The acceptance scenario in US-1 clarifies this, but FR-002 should be self-contained for verifiability.

Constitution VI (Human Subjects Ethics) requires IRB approval and informed consent workflow before participant testing. Spec mentions 'validated question format from existing literature' but does not address ethics approval, consent forms, or PII handling workflow. This is a constitutional requirement for any human-subjects experiment.

Idea validation mentions 'recognition and recall tests' but spec only implements recognition-style questions (20 questions about scene details). If recall is required by the research design, this is under-reach. Clarify whether recall testing is in-scope or if recognition-only is sufficient for the stated research question.

Constitution VII (Stimulus Standardization) requires metadata file recording manipulation parameters (detail level, object list, texture settings) for all stimuli. FR-002 mentions creating manipulated versions but does not explicitly require metadata documentation. Add explicit requirement for stimulus metadata storage.

FR-004 defines 'false details (present only in enhanced/reduced versions)' — if participants view manipulated images, these are REAL details they saw, not false memories. This creates a circular/tautological definition where the manipulation itself determines what counts as 'false memory'.

The false memory measurement design is conceptually inverted. Standard visual false memory paradigms test whether participants remember details from a BASELINE image that were NOT present. Here, 'false' details are defined as those added/removed in manipulation, but participants only see manipulated versions — they cannot distinguish baseline from manipulation.

Repeated-measures ANOVA requires same participants in all conditions, but SC-002 specifies '≥50 per condition' suggesting between-subjects design. This is a methodological mismatch that invalidates the stated analysis plan.

Assumption states 'DRM paradigm adaptations for visual stimuli' — DRM is for semantic/verbal false memory (word lists), not visual detail false memory. This may be a category error requiring different validated paradigms (e.g., misinformation effect, source monitoring paradigms).

SC-002 states 'Statistical power is measured against the planned sample size' — power is calculated BEFORE data collection based on effect size estimates, not measured after. This reverses the causal/temporal relationship and is conceptually incorrect.

Constitution Principle VI requires IRB/ethics approval for human subjects research, but spec contains no mention of ethics compliance, consent procedures, or IRB documentation. This is a fundamental violation for psychology research with human participants.

new_concerns.text: US-1 creates enhanced/reduced detail images but US-2 only has participants view BASELINE image. The visual detail manipulation exists but is never presented to participants, so the design cannot test the research question about visual detail's impact on false memory. Need to specify which condition(s) participants view.

Repeated-measures ANOVA requires same participants in all conditions, but the design appears between-subjects (each participant sees one image). If participants only view one condition, between-subjects ANOVA or t-test is appropriate, not repeated-measures.
